expositionText: I've been keeping the grain stores stocked for {settlement_name} on my own for about two years now. Nobody asked me to. I just noticed it needed doing. I'm short {gather_count} {quest_item} and I could use a hand getting them.
acceptText: Appreciated. I'll put them to good use.
declineText: Fair enough. I'll get to it when I get to it.
expositionTurnInText: That'll do nicely. The stores are looking better than they have in a while. There's one more thing that would help, if you have the time.
conflict1Text: I've been meaning to get word to {target_npc} in {target_npc_settlement}. They're a {target_npc_role} who helped me set this whole effort up years back. I'd like them to know the work is still going. Would you pass that along?
conflict1TurnInText: Good to hear. I imagine {target_npc} was surprised it was still running. Most people figured I'd have quit by now.
resolutionText: The stores are full and the word is out. That's a better position than I've been in for months. Take {quest_reward}. The work goes on either way, but it's easier knowing someone noticed.

skillCheck.passText: I suppose the honest part is that I've thought about stopping more than once. Not because I don't believe in it. Because it gets quiet doing something nobody thanks you for. But then the winter comes and the stores are there, and that's answer enough.
skillCheck.failText: It's steady work. That's about the size of it.

rewardText: a fair day's wage and a sack of good flour
targetNpcOpener: Someone came all the way out here to tell me the grain stores are still going? Well. I always figured that one would outlast the rest of us. Stubborn runs deep in that family.
targetNpcCloser: Tell them I said to keep at it. And to eat something themselves once in a while.

expositionText: I patrol the east road every morning and evening. Most days nothing happens, but the {enemy_type_plural} have been showing up more often and I can't cover the ground alone. I need {kill_count} of them cleared out so the path stays open for the traders.
acceptText: Good. That frees me up to handle the other side.
expositionTurnInText: The road's quieter already. I walked it this morning without seeing a single track. That's the first time in weeks. There's one more thing I've been putting off.
conflict1Text: I've been needing a {quest_item} to replace the one I wore through. Hard to keep a daily patrol going with worn-out gear. If one turns up in your travels, I'd be glad to have it.
conflict1TurnInText: That'll last me a good while. Solid piece of work.
resolutionText: The road is open, my kit is sorted, and the job goes on. Take {quest_reward}. I set it aside for exactly this kind of help.

skillCheck.passText: People ask me why I bother when nobody pays me for it. The answer is that someone walked this road for me when I was young and couldn't walk it myself. I'm just returning what was given.
skillCheck.failText: Somebody has to do it. Might as well be me.

rewardText: what I've put aside from my own stores

expositionText: My mother needs medicine from {other_settlement}, but the {enemy_type_plural} along the road have made it too risky for me to go myself. If you could deal with {kill_count} of them, I can make the trip safely.
acceptText: I appreciate that. She's waiting on me and I don't like making her wait.
declineText: All right. I'll find a way around them. It'll just take longer.
expositionTurnInText: Good. The road should be passable now. I'll head out in the morning. But there's someone I need to reach before I go.
conflict1Text: My cousin {target_npc} lives in {target_npc_settlement}. They've been looking after her when I can't. I'd like you to let {target_npc_role} know I'm on my way, so they can have things ready.
conflict1TurnInText: {target_npc} knows I'm coming? Good. That means she won't be alone tonight while I'm still traveling.
resolutionText: I got to her in time. She's doing better already, and she told me I looked tired, which means she's feeling well enough to worry about someone else. Take {quest_reward}. You made a hard week simpler.

skillCheck.passText: She raised me on her own after my father left. I don't talk about it much, but every time she needs something and I can't get there fast enough, I feel like I'm failing at the one thing that matters.
skillCheck.failText: She's family. You do what you can.

rewardText: a small purse and a good meal for the road
targetNpcOpener: You're here about the medicine run? Good. She's been asking after the trip every morning. I've been telling her it's in hand, so I'm glad that's finally true.
targetNpcCloser: I'll have everything laid out before nightfall. Tell them not to rush on my account.

expositionText: My brother took a bad fall last week and he can't work his trade until his hands heal. I've been covering for him, but I need a {quest_item} to keep things running. He'd never ask anyone for help himself, so I'm doing the asking.
acceptText: Thank you. He'll grumble about it, but he'll be better off.
declineText: I understand. I'll sort it out. I usually do.
expositionTurnInText: That's exactly what I needed. His workshop is back in order, at least for the next stretch. There's still the matter of keeping it that way.
conflict1Text: The {enemy_type_plural} have been getting bolder around the property. He can't fend them off right now and I can't be there every hour of the day. Take {kill_count} of them down and we'll both sleep easier.
conflict1TurnInText: That's a weight off. He actually sat outside this afternoon for the first time since the fall. One more thing, if you're willing.
conflict2Text: His old apprentice {target_npc} moved to {target_npc_settlement} last season. I think hearing from {target_npc_role} would do him good. Would you let them know he's mending and could use a letter?
conflict2TurnInText: {target_npc} is going to write? That'll mean more to him than he'd ever admit.
resolutionText: His hands are healing. He's complaining about being idle, which means he's almost himself again. Take {quest_reward}. You helped me keep my brother's life in one piece while he couldn't do it himself.

skillCheck.passText: He's proud, always has been. The hardest part isn't the work itself, it's doing it quietly enough that he doesn't feel diminished. I'd rather wear myself out than let him think he's a burden.
skillCheck.failText: He's mending. That's the important part.

rewardText: what we can spare between us and honest thanks
targetNpcOpener: Is he all right? I heard about the fall from a trader passing through, but nobody could tell me how bad it was. I've been meaning to send word but I didn't know what to say.
targetNpcCloser: I'll write tonight. Tell him I said his grip was always too strong for his own good.

expositionText: My son needs to leave {settlement_name}. There's nothing for him here that won't get him hurt eventually, and I've made my peace with that. The {enemy_type_plural} between here and the north road need thinning first. {kill_count} of them gone and I can put him on a wagon without worrying he won't arrive.
acceptText: Good. I've been putting this off and I shouldn't have.
declineText: I'll find another way. There's always another way, even if it takes longer.
expositionTurnInText: The road is safer now. I checked it myself this morning. There's one arrangement left to make before he goes.
conflict1Text: {target_npc} in {target_npc_settlement} agreed to take him in, years ago. I need you to tell {target_npc_role} the time has come. They'll know what it means.
conflict1TurnInText: Then it's settled. I thought I'd feel worse about it being real. Instead I just feel like I can finally stop planning and start doing.
resolutionText: He left this morning. Didn't look back, which is exactly what I told him to do. Take {quest_reward}. I'd rather he grow up somewhere safe than keep him here for my own comfort.

skillCheck.passText: I've known this was coming for two years. I watched this place get harder and I weighed it out every season. The numbers stopped working in his favor a while back. It's arithmetic, not emotion.
skillCheck.failText: It's the right call. That's enough for me.

rewardText: a fair sum I set aside for this day
targetNpcOpener: I've been expecting this message for a while now. Honestly, I'm surprised it took this long. The room's been ready since autumn.
targetNpcCloser: Tell them the boy will be looked after. That's a promise, not a courtesy.

expositionText: My wife is going to stay with her family in {other_settlement} while I sort things out here. She needs a {quest_item} for the journey. I've looked, but I can't find one nearby, and there's no time to search further. If you come across one, I'd be grateful.
acceptText: Thank you. She'll travel better with that in hand.
declineText: I'll keep looking. Something will turn up.
expositionTurnInText: That's sorted. She can pack properly now. There's still the matter of getting her there safely.
conflict1Text: The {enemy_type_plural} along the south trail have been thick this month. I need {kill_count} of them cleared before I'll feel right about sending her that direction.
conflict1TurnInText: Good. I walked it after you finished and didn't see anything moving. One more thing to arrange.
conflict2Text: Her brother {target_npc} lives in {target_npc_settlement}. I'd like you to let {target_npc_role} know she's coming and when to expect her. They'll want to prepare.
conflict2TurnInText: {target_npc} is ready for her? Then there's nothing left to wait on.
resolutionText: She left at first light and we didn't make a scene of it. She said she'd write when she arrived, and I said I'd read it twice. Take {quest_reward}. This was always the practical choice, and you helped me make it cleanly.

skillCheck.passText: The truth is, she wanted to stay. I'm the one who insisted. It wasn't an argument, just a conversation where I had the worse facts and she couldn't answer them. That's a hard thing to be right about.
skillCheck.failText: We talked it through. The decision was made together.

rewardText: what I can spare and a handshake worth something
targetNpcOpener: She's finally coming? I won't pretend I'm surprised. The letters she's been sending got shorter every month, and I could read between the lines well enough.
targetNpcCloser: The spare room is hers for as long as she needs it. No questions from my end.

expositionText: My father passed a few months back. I've handled most of the arrangements, but there's a {quest_item} he always kept with him that I'd like to put on the shelf with his other things. If you find one in your travels, I'd take it as a kindness.
acceptText: I appreciate it. It's a small thing, but it's the last one on the list.
declineText: No trouble. I'll find one eventually. There's no rush anymore.
expositionTurnInText: That's the one. Looks about right sitting there next to his other things. There's one more matter I've been meaning to see to.
conflict1Text: His old partner {target_npc} in {target_npc_settlement} should hear the full story. I sent a letter, but letters don't carry much weight for this kind of news. Would you talk to {target_npc_role} in person?
conflict1TurnInText: Thank you. {target_npc} deserved to hear it from a person, and I wasn't ready to make that trip myself.
resolutionText: The shelf looks right now, and the people who needed to know have been told. Take {quest_reward}. He was a practical man, and he'd have approved of paying someone fairly for finishing the job.

skillCheck.passText: I thought I'd feel more settled by now, but grief doesn't follow a schedule. Some mornings I forget for a minute, and then I remember, and it's just as fresh. I'm getting through the days, and that counts for something.
skillCheck.failText: It's been a few months. I'm managing.

rewardText: a few of his coins that should go to someone who earned them
targetNpcOpener: I had a feeling someone would come with this news sooner or later. He was the kind of man who'd have worked right up until the end, so I imagine that's close to what happened.
targetNpcCloser: I'm glad I know. Pass along my respects, if you would.

expositionText: I lost my sister over the winter. I've been getting on with things since then, which is what she would have expected. But the {enemy_type_plural} have been getting close to {settlement_name} and I can't focus on anything else until {kill_count} of them are dealt with.
acceptText: Good. That frees me up to take care of the things I've been putting off.
declineText: I'll handle it. I just need to find the time between everything else.
expositionTurnInText: Quieter now. I can hear myself think again, which is the first step toward getting the rest of this sorted. There's something I've been meaning to do for her.
conflict1Text: She kept a {quest_item} on her workbench for as long as I can remember. I'd like to find one and put it there again. The bench looks wrong without it.
conflict1TurnInText: That's right. That's where it goes. The bench looks like hers again, and that's enough.
conflict2Text: One last thing. Her friend {target_npc} in {target_npc_settlement} has been asking about her in letters I haven't had the will to answer. Would you go see {target_npc_role} and tell them what happened? I'd rather they hear it straight.
conflict2TurnInText: Then {target_npc} knows. I should have done that sooner, but I'm glad it's done now.
resolutionText: Her bench is set right, her friend has been told, and the road is quiet. That's about as settled as things get. Take {quest_reward}. She would have called it practical, and she would have meant it as a compliment.

skillCheck.passText: I keep her tools in order every morning before I start my own work. I know it's not necessary. I think it's the closest thing I have to a conversation with her anymore, and I'm not ready to give that up.
skillCheck.failText: We were close. That's about all there is to say.

rewardText: a share of what she left me and my honest thanks
targetNpcOpener: I was wondering why the letters stopped. I'd hoped it was just a busy season, but I suppose I knew better than that. She wasn't the type to forget.
targetNpcCloser: Thank you for coming in person. A letter would have felt wrong for this.

expositionText: I've been planning an expansion to my workshop for about six months. Nothing fancy, just enough room to take on bigger commissions. I need {gather_count} {quest_item} to get started on the foundation. I can pay for the help.
acceptText: Good. I've had the plans drawn up for weeks. Time to put them to use.
declineText: No matter. I'll gather them myself. It'll just push the timeline back.
expositionTurnInText: That's a solid start. The foundation is going to hold well with this much on hand. There's one more piece I need before I can really get moving.
conflict1Text: I need a {quest_item} for the main workstation. It's the one thing I can't substitute and I haven't been able to source it locally. If you find one, the whole project comes together.
conflict1TurnInText: That's exactly what I was after. The workstation is going to be twice as capable as the old one.
resolutionText: The expansion is underway. By next month I'll be taking commissions I used to have to turn down, and that's a good feeling. Take {quest_reward}. You helped me take a plan off paper and put it into wood and stone.

skillCheck.passText: I'll tell you the part I don't say out loud. I want to build something that outlasts me. Not because I need anyone to remember my name, but because I'd like to know the work continues after I'm done with it.
skillCheck.failText: It's a good project. That's reason enough.

rewardText: a fair wage and first pick of anything I craft this season

expositionText: I want to set up a proper trade route between {settlement_name} and {target_npc_settlement}, not the informal kind but a real one with regular schedules. {target_npc} is a {target_npc_role} over there who could make it work. Would you go introduce the idea?
acceptText: Good. I've been sitting on this plan long enough.
declineText: I'll find another way to pitch it. The idea is sound, it just needs the right messenger.
expositionTurnInText: {target_npc} is interested? I thought they might be. The numbers work for both sides. Now I need to hold up my end.
conflict1Text: I promised I'd have a first shipment ready as proof of concept. I need {gather_count} {quest_item} to fill the order. Bring me that and I can show them this is more than talk.
conflict1TurnInText: That's a respectable first load. Enough to prove the route is worth running. One more thing standing in the way.
conflict2Text: The {enemy_type_plural} along the middle stretch have been bad enough to scare off the last two couriers I tried to hire. I need {kill_count} of them put down so the route is actually viable.
conflict2TurnInText: Good. I'll send the shipment tomorrow with a clear conscience. Nobody needs to risk their neck for a crate of goods.
resolutionText: The first shipment went through clean. {target_npc} sent back payment and a note asking when the next one arrives. Take {quest_reward}. You helped me turn an idea into something real, and that's the hardest step.

skillCheck.passText: My father ran a route like this before the roads got bad. He always said the trick was starting small and being reliable. I've been reliable my whole life. I just needed the small start.
skillCheck.failText: It's a sound plan. The details will sort themselves.

rewardText: a percentage of the first shipment's earnings and my thanks
targetNpcOpener: A trade route with regular schedules? I've been hoping someone on that end would finally put it together. The demand is here, I just needed someone steady enough to supply it.
targetNpcCloser: Tell them I'm in. Send the first load and we'll see how it goes from there.
